'en'} * {'text': '三重県鈴鹿市一宮町', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} [[EN:Tsubaki Shrine]] [[JA:都波岐神社・奈加等神社]] [[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年10月}} {{神社 |名称 = 都波岐神社・奈加等神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}一ノ宮町1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=都波岐神社・奈加等神社}} |祭神 = 都波岐神社：{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦大神}}
奈加等神社：天椹野命、{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年 |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=都波岐神社
奈加等神社|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鳥居]] '''都波岐神社・奈加等神社'''（つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === 両社とも{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。都波岐神社は{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}とされる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に都波岐神社と奈加等神社を合併したもので、'''都波岐奈加等神社'''とも称される。 === 祭神 === ; 都波岐神社 * {{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦大神}}（さるたひこのおおかみ） ; 奈加等神社 * 天椹野命（あまのくののみこと） - 中跡直（なかとのあたい）の先祖。 * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}}（なかつつおのみこと） === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年、{{jalink|勅|Q905765}}により{{jalink|伊勢国造|Q11378974}}の高雄束命{{jalink|猿田彦}}の八世の孫とされる。が{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|河曲郡|Q11553861}}{{jalink|中跡村}}（現在地）に社殿を2つ造営し、それぞれ都波岐神社・奈加等神社と称したのが起源とされる。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では両社とも小社に列した。{{jalink|天長|Q1373138}}年間に{{jalink|空海|Q222227|弘法大師（空海）}}が参籠し、{{jalink|獅子頭}}2つを{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}した。{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}3年（{{jalink|1079年|Q19445}}）、2社に対して{{jalink|正一位|Q11123258}}の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられ、{{jalink|白河天皇|Q60949}}より{{jalink|宸翰|Q11455596|宸筆}}の{{jalink|勅額|Q11071212}}が授けられた。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の伊勢平定の際に社殿を焼失し、旧記も失われた。{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}年間、{{jalink|神戸城|Q11589845}}主一柳監物が再建した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に両社をあわせて「都波岐神社奈加等神社」として{{jalink|県社}}に列格。現在は2社の{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}が1つの社殿に{{jalink|祭神#主神・配神|Q11591100|相殿}}として祀られる。 === 主な祭事 === * {{jalink|例大祭}}、中戸{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}（10月10日） === 所在地 === * {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}一ノ宮町1181 === 交通アクセス === ; 路線バス * {{jalink|河原田駅|Q4559716}}（{{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|関西線 (名古屋地区)|Q11656377|関西本線}}・{{jalink|伊勢鉄道|Q11379148}}{{jalink|伊勢鉄道伊勢線|Q6079603|伊勢線}}）または{{jalink|鈴鹿市駅|Q11649493}}（{{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|近鉄鈴鹿線|Q900210|鈴鹿線}}）から、{{jalink|三重交通|Q6843516}}バスで「高岡」バス停下車、徒歩で約10分。 ; 自家用車 * {{jalink|国道23号|Q5357579}}鈴鹿大橋から南約1キロメートルの一ノ宮町{{jalink|交差点|Q285783}}近く。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 関連項目 === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; 猿田彦大神を祀る神社 * {{jalink|椿大神社|Q3541617}} - 伊勢国一宮を称するもう1つの神社。 * {{jalink|猿田彦神社|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{Expand Japanese|都波岐神社・奈加等神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Shrine | native_name = 都波岐神社・奈加等神社 | image = Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Tsubaki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.901245|136.601281}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''|都波岐神社・奈加等神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. 'en'} * {'text': '三重県鈴鹿市一宮町', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} [[EN:Tsubaki Shrine]] [[JA:都波岐神社・奈加等神社]] [[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年10月}} {{神社 |名称 = 都波岐神社・奈加等神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}一ノ宮町1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=都波岐神社・奈加等神社}} |祭神 = 都波岐神社：{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦大神}}
奈加等神社：天椹野命、{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年 |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=都波岐神社
奈加等神社|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鳥居]] '''都波岐神社・奈加等神社'''（つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === 両社とも{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。都波岐神社は{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}とされる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に都波岐神社と奈加等神社を合併したもので、'''都波岐奈加等神社'''とも称される。 === 祭神 === ; 都波岐神社 * {{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦大神}}（さるたひこのおおかみ） ; 奈加等神社 * 天椹野命（あまのくののみこと） - 中跡直（なかとのあたい）の先祖。 * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}}（なかつつおのみこと） === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年、{{jalink|勅|Q905765}}により{{jalink|伊勢国造|Q11378974}}の高雄束命{{jalink|猿田彦}}の八世の孫とされる。が{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|河曲郡|Q11553861}}{{jalink|中跡村}}（現在地）に社殿を2つ造営し、それぞれ都波岐神社・奈加等神社と称したのが起源とされる。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では両社とも小社に列した。{{jalink|天長|Q1373138}}年間に{{jalink|空海|Q222227|弘法大師（空海）}}が参籠し、{{jalink|獅子頭}}2つを{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}した。{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}3年（{{jalink|1079年|Q19445}}）、2社に対して{{jalink|正一位|Q11123258}}の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられ、{{jalink|白河天皇|Q60949}}より{{jalink|宸翰|Q11455596|宸筆}}の{{jalink|勅額|Q11071212}}が授けられた。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の伊勢平定の際に社殿を焼失し、旧記も失われた。{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}年間、{{jalink|神戸城|Q11589845}}主一柳監物が再建した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に両社をあわせて「都波岐神社奈加等神社」として{{jalink|県社}}に列格。現在は2社の{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}が1つの社殿に{{jalink|祭神#主神・配神|Q11591100|相殿}}として祀られる。 === 主な祭事 === * {{jalink|例大祭}}、中戸{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}（10月10日） === 所在地 === * {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}一ノ宮町1181 === 交通アクセス === ; 路線バス * {{jalink|河原田駅|Q4559716}}（{{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|関西線 (名古屋地区)|Q11656377|関西本線}}・{{jalink|伊勢鉄道|Q11379148}}{{jalink|伊勢鉄道伊勢線|Q6079603|伊勢線}}）または{{jalink|鈴鹿市駅|Q11649493}}（{{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|近鉄鈴鹿線|Q900210|鈴鹿線}}）から、{{jalink|三重交通|Q6843516}}バスで「高岡」バス停下車、徒歩で約10分。 ; 自家用車 * {{jalink|国道23号|Q5357579}}鈴鹿大橋から南約1キロメートルの一ノ宮町{{jalink|交差点|Q285783}}近く。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 関連項目 === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; 猿田彦大神を祀る神社 * {{jalink|椿大神社|Q3541617}} - 伊勢国一宮を称するもう1つの神社。 * {{jalink|猿田彦神社|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

[[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年10月}} {{神社 |名称 = 都波岐神社・奈加等神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}一ノ宮町1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=都波岐神社・奈加等神社}} |祭神 = 都波岐神社：{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦大神}}
奈加等神社：天椹野命、{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年 |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=都波岐神社
奈加等神社|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鳥居]] '''都波岐神社・奈加等神社'''（つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === 両社とも{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。都波岐神社は{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}とされる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に都波岐神社と奈加等神社を合併したもので、'''都波岐奈加等神社'''とも称される。 === 祭神 === ; 都波岐神社 * {{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦大神}}（さるたひこのおおかみ） ; 奈加等神社 * 天椹野命（あまのくののみこと） - 中跡直（なかとのあたい）の先祖。 * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}}（なかつつおのみこと） === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年、{{jalink|勅|Q905765}}により{{jalink|伊勢国造|Q11378974}}の高雄束命{{jalink|猿田彦}}の八世の孫とされる。が{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|河曲郡|Q11553861}}{{jalink|中跡村}}（現在地）に社殿を2つ造営し、それぞれ都波岐神社・奈加等神社と称したのが起源とされる。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では両社とも小社に列した。{{jalink|天長|Q1373138}}年間に{{jalink|空海|Q222227|弘法大師（空海）}}が参籠し、{{jalink|獅子頭}}2つを{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}した。{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}3年（{{jalink|1079年|Q19445}}）、2社に対して{{jalink|正一位|Q11123258}}の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられ、{{jalink|白河天皇|Q60949}}より{{jalink|宸翰|Q11455596|宸筆}}の{{jalink|勅額|Q11071212}}が授けられた。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の伊勢平定の際に社殿を焼失し、旧記も失われた。{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}年間、{{jalink|神戸城|Q11589845}}主一柳監物が再建した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に両社をあわせて「都波岐神社奈加等神社」として{{jalink|県社}}に列格。現在は2社の{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}が1つの社殿に{{jalink|祭神#主神・配神|Q11591100|相殿}}として祀られる。 === 主な祭事 === * {{jalink|例大祭}}、中戸{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}（10月10日） === 所在地 === * {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}一ノ宮町1181 === 交通アクセス === ; 路線バス * {{jalink|河原田駅|Q4559716}}（{{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|関西線 (名古屋地区)|Q11656377|関西本線}}・{{jalink|伊勢鉄道|Q11379148}}{{jalink|伊勢鉄道伊勢線|Q6079603|伊勢線}}）または{{jalink|鈴鹿市駅|Q11649493}}（{{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|近鉄鈴鹿線|Q900210|鈴鹿線}}）から、{{jalink|三重交通|Q6843516}}バスで「高岡」バス停下車、徒歩で約10分。 ; 自家用車 * {{jalink|国道23号|Q5357579}}鈴鹿大橋から南約1キロメートルの一ノ宮町{{jalink|交差点|Q285783}}近く。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 関連項目 === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; 猿田彦大神を祀る神社 * {{jalink|椿大神社|Q3541617}} - 伊勢国一宮を称するもう1つの神社。 * {{jalink|猿田彦神社|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

奈加等神社：天椹野命、{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年 |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=都波岐神社
奈加等神社|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鳥居]] '''都波岐神社・奈加等神社'''（つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === 両社とも{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。都波岐神社は{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}とされる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に都波岐神社と奈加等神社を合併したもので、'''都波岐奈加等神社'''とも称される。 === 祭神 === ; 都波岐神社 * {{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦大神}}（さるたひこのおおかみ） ; 奈加等神社 * 天椹野命（あまのくののみこと） - 中跡直（なかとのあたい）の先祖。 * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|中筒之男命}}（なかつつおのみこと） === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}23年、{{jalink|勅|Q905765}}により{{jalink|伊勢国造|Q11378974}}の高雄束命{{jalink|猿田彦}}の八世の孫とされる。が{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}{{jalink|河曲郡|Q11553861}}{{jalink|中跡村}}（現在地）に社殿を2つ造営し、それぞれ都波岐神社・奈加等神社と称したのが起源とされる。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では両社とも小社に列した。{{jalink|天長|Q1373138}}年間に{{jalink|空海|Q222227|弘法大師（空海）}}が参籠し、{{jalink|獅子頭}}2つを{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}した。{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}3年（{{jalink|1079年|Q19445}}）、2社に対して{{jalink|正一位|Q11123258}}の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられ、{{jalink|白河天皇|Q60949}}より{{jalink|宸翰|Q11455596|宸筆}}の{{jalink|勅額|Q11071212}}が授けられた。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の伊勢平定の際に社殿を焼失し、旧記も失われた。{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}年間、{{jalink|神戸城|Q11589845}}主一柳監物が再建した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代に両社をあわせて「都波岐神社奈加等神社」として{{jalink|県社}}に列格。現在は2社の{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}が1つの社殿に{{jalink|祭神#主神・配神|Q11591100|相殿}}として祀られる。 === 主な祭事 === * {{jalink|例大祭}}、中戸{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}（10月10日） === 所在地 === * {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}一ノ宮町1181 === 交通アクセス === ; 路線バス * {{jalink|河原田駅|Q4559716}}（{{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|関西線 (名古屋地区)|Q11656377|関西本線}}・{{jalink|伊勢鉄道|Q11379148}}{{jalink|伊勢鉄道伊勢線|Q6079603|伊勢線}}）または{{jalink|鈴鹿市駅|Q11649493}}（{{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|近鉄鈴鹿線|Q900210|鈴鹿線}}）から、{{jalink|三重交通|Q6843516}}バスで「高岡」バス停下車、徒歩で約10分。 ; 自家用車 * {{jalink|国道23号|Q5357579}}鈴鹿大橋から南約1キロメートルの一ノ宮町{{jalink|交差点|Q285783}}近く。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 関連項目 === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; 猿田彦大神を祀る神社 * {{jalink|椿大神社|Q3541617}} - 伊勢国一宮を称するもう1つの神社。 * {{jalink|猿田彦神社|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

'en'} * {'text': '三重県鈴鹿市一宮町', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} [[EN:Tsubaki Shrine]] [[JA:都波岐神社・奈加等神社]] [[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and their former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}, by {{jalink|imperial order|Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Kawawa District|Q11553861}} {{jalink|Nakato Village}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{jalink|Tenchō|Q1373138}}, {{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227|Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} two {{jalink|lion heads}}. In {{jalink|Shōryaku|Q279743}} 3 ({{jalink|1079|Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{jalink|Junior First Rank|Q11123258}} in {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{jalink|chokugaku|Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{jalink|Kobe Castle|Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{jalink|kensha}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{jalink|associated hall deities|Q11591100|aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{jalink|Grand annual festival}}, Nakato {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{jalink|Kawarada Station|Q4559716}} ({{jalink|JR Central|Q513679|JR}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|Kansai Main Line}}, {{jalink|Ise Railway|Q11379148}} {{jalink|Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|Ise Line}}) or {{jalink|Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493}} ({{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Suzuka Line|Q900210|Suzuka Line}}), take {{jalink|Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{jalink|National Route 23|Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

'en'} * {'text': '三重県鈴鹿市一宮町', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} [[EN:Tsubaki Shrine]] [[JA:都波岐神社・奈加等神社]] [[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and their former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}, by {{jalink|imperial order|Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Kawawa District|Q11553861}} {{jalink|Nakato Village}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{jalink|Tenchō|Q1373138}}, {{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227|Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} two {{jalink|lion heads}}. In {{jalink|Shōryaku|Q279743}} 3 ({{jalink|1079|Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{jalink|Junior First Rank|Q11123258}} in {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{jalink|chokugaku|Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{jalink|Kobe Castle|Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{jalink|kensha}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{jalink|associated hall deities|Q11591100|aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{jalink|Grand annual festival}}, Nakato {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{jalink|Kawarada Station|Q4559716}} ({{jalink|JR Central|Q513679|JR}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|Kansai Main Line}}, {{jalink|Ise Railway|Q11379148}} {{jalink|Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|Ise Line}}) or {{jalink|Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493}} ({{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Suzuka Line|Q900210|Suzuka Line}}), take {{jalink|Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{jalink|National Route 23|Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

[[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and their former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}, by {{jalink|imperial order|Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Kawawa District|Q11553861}} {{jalink|Nakato Village}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{jalink|Tenchō|Q1373138}}, {{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227|Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} two {{jalink|lion heads}}. In {{jalink|Shōryaku|Q279743}} 3 ({{jalink|1079|Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{jalink|Junior First Rank|Q11123258}} in {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{jalink|chokugaku|Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{jalink|Kobe Castle|Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{jalink|kensha}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{jalink|associated hall deities|Q11591100|aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{jalink|Grand annual festival}}, Nakato {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{jalink|Kawarada Station|Q4559716}} ({{jalink|JR Central|Q513679|JR}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|Kansai Main Line}}, {{jalink|Ise Railway|Q11379148}} {{jalink|Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|Ise Line}}) or {{jalink|Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493}} ({{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Suzuka Line|Q900210|Suzuka Line}}), take {{jalink|Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{jalink|National Route 23|Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and their former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}, by {{jalink|imperial order|Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Kawawa District|Q11553861}} {{jalink|Nakato Village}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{jalink|Tenchō|Q1373138}}, {{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227|Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} two {{jalink|lion heads}}. In {{jalink|Shōryaku|Q279743}} 3 ({{jalink|1079|Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{jalink|Junior First Rank|Q11123258}} in {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{jalink|chokugaku|Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{jalink|Kobe Castle|Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{jalink|kensha}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{jalink|associated hall deities|Q11591100|aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{jalink|Grand annual festival}}, Nakato {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{jalink|Kawarada Station|Q4559716}} ({{jalink|JR Central|Q513679|JR}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|Kansai Main Line}}, {{jalink|Ise Railway|Q11379148}} {{jalink|Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|Ise Line}}) or {{jalink|Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493}} ({{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Suzuka Line|Q900210|Suzuka Line}}), take {{jalink|Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{jalink|National Route 23|Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and their former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}, by {{jalink|imperial order|Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Kawawa District|Q11553861}} {{jalink|Nakato Village}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{jalink|Tenchō|Q1373138}}, {{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227|Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} two {{jalink|lion heads}}. In {{jalink|Shōryaku|Q279743}} 3 ({{jalink|1079|Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{jalink|Junior First Rank|Q11123258}} in {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{jalink|chokugaku|Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{jalink|Kobe Castle|Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{jalink|kensha}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{jalink|associated hall deities|Q11591100|aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{jalink|Grand annual festival}}, Nakato {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{jalink|Kawarada Station|Q4559716}} ({{jalink|JR Central|Q513679|JR}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|Kansai Main Line}}, {{jalink|Ise Railway|Q11379148}} {{jalink|Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|Ise Line}}) or {{jalink|Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493}} ({{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Suzuka Line|Q900210|Suzuka Line}}), take {{jalink|Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{jalink|National Route 23|Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]]

former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and their former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}, by {{jalink|imperial order|Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Kawawa District|Q11553861}} {{jalink|Nakato Village}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{jalink|Tenchō|Q1373138}}, {{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227|Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} two {{jalink|lion heads}}. In {{jalink|Shōryaku|Q279743}} 3 ({{jalink|1079|Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{jalink|Junior First Rank|Q11123258}} in {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{jalink|chokugaku|Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{jalink|Kobe Castle|Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{jalink|kensha}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{jalink|associated hall deities|Q11591100|aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{jalink|Grand annual festival}}, Nakato {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{jalink|Kawarada Station|Q4559716}} ({{jalink|JR Central|Q513679|JR}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|Kansai Main Line}}, {{jalink|Ise Railway|Q11379148}} {{jalink|Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|Ise Line}}) or {{jalink|Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493}} ({{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Suzuka Line|Q900210|Suzuka Line}}), take {{jalink|Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{jalink|National Route 23|Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and their former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}, by {{jalink|imperial order|Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Kawawa District|Q11553861}} {{jalink|Nakato Village}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{jalink|Tenchō|Q1373138}}, {{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227|Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} two {{jalink|lion heads}}. In {{jalink|Shōryaku|Q279743}} 3 ({{jalink|1079|Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{jalink|Junior First Rank|Q11123258}} in {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{jalink|chokugaku|Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{jalink|Kobe Castle|Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{jalink|kensha}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{jalink|associated hall deities|Q11591100|aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{jalink|Grand annual festival}}, Nakato {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{jalink|Kawarada Station|Q4559716}} ({{jalink|JR Central|Q513679|JR}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|Kansai Main Line}}, {{jalink|Ise Railway|Q11379148}} {{jalink|Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|Ise Line}}) or {{jalink|Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493}} ({{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Suzuka Line|Q900210|Suzuka Line}}), take {{jalink|Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{jalink|National Route 23|Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine|Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}} {{ill||lt=Nakato Village|UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788}} two {{ill||lt=lion heads|UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill||lt=Grand annual festival|UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakato Village|UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788}} two {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion heads|UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand annual festival|UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'en'} * {'text': '三重県鈴鹿市一宮町', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} [[EN:Tsubaki Shrine]] [[JA:都波岐神社・奈加等神社]] [[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakato Village|1=UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788}} two {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion heads|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand annual festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakato Village|1=UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788}} two {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion heads|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand annual festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakato Village|1=UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788}} two {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion heads|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand annual festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしやなかとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
