needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765|1=Command paper}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861|1=Kawawa district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakato Village|1=UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)|1=Kūkai}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} two {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion heads|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743|1=Jōryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445|1=1079}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949|1=Emperor Shirakawa}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212|1=imperial plaque}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845|1=Kanbe Castle}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono|1=Saijin}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand annual festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716|1=Kawarada Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148|1=Ise Railway}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line|1=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493|1=Suzukashi Station}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line|1=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579|1=Japan National Route 23}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617|1=Tsubaki Grand Shrine}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141|1=Sarutahiko Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Suzuka, Mie]]

Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} [[EN:Tsubaki Shrine]] [[JA:都波岐神社・奈加等神社]] [[ZH:都波岐奈加等神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11643733}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765|1=Command paper}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861|1=Kawawa district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakato Village|1=UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)|1=Kūkai}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} two {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion heads|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743|1=Jōryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445|1=1079}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949|1=Emperor Shirakawa}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212|1=imperial plaque}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845|1=Kanbe Castle}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono|1=Saijin}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand annual festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716|1=Kawarada Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148|1=Ise Railway}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line|1=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493|1=Suzukashi Station}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line|1=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579|1=Japan National Route 23}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617|1=Tsubaki Grand Shrine}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141|1=Sarutahiko Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Suzuka, Mie]]

needed|date=October 2019}} {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Shaden1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|04.5|N|136|36|04.5|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsubaki Shrine: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
Nakato Shrine: Amanokuno-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=4.5|lon_deg=136|lon_min=36|lon_sec=4.5|label=Tsubaki Shrine
Nakato Shrine|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[ファイル:Tsubaki-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Tsubaki Shrine / Nakato Shrine''' (つばきじんじゃ・なかとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. === Overview === Both are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and their former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Tsubaki Shrine is regarded as an {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. They were merged in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and are also called '''Tsubaki Nakato Shrine'''. === Enshrined deities === ; Tsubaki Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのおおかみ) ; Nakato Shrine * Amanokuno-no-Mikoto - ancestor of Nakato no Atai. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (なかつつおのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, in year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, by {{ill|lt=imperial order|Q905765|WD=Q905765|1=Command paper}}, Takao Tsuka-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11378974|WD=Q11378974|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built two shrine buildings in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861|1=Kawawa district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakato Village|1=UNKNOWN}} (current location), naming them Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' both are listed as small shrines. During {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}}, {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)|1=Kūkai}} entered seclusion here and {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} two {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion heads|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743|1=Jōryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1079|Q19445|WD=Q19445|1=1079}}), both shrines were granted {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|Q60949|WD=Q60949|1=Emperor Shirakawa}} granted an imperial-calligraphy {{ill|lt=chokugaku|Q11071212|WD=Q11071212|1=imperial plaque}} plaque. In the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} years, shrine buildings burned during {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}'s pacification of Ise and old records were lost. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} years, they were rebuilt by Ichiyanagi Kenmotsu, lord of {{ill|lt=Kobe Castle|Q11589845|WD=Q11589845|1=Kanbe Castle}}. In Meiji, both shrines were combined and ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} as "Tsubaki Shrine Nakato Shrine." Today the deities of both are enshrined as {{ill|lt=associated hall deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=aidono|1=Saijin}} in one shrine building. === Main festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand annual festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nakato {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} (October 10) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}Ichinomiya-chō 1181 === Access === ; Route bus * From {{ill|lt=Kawarada Station|Q4559716|WD=Q4559716|1=Kawarada Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11656377|WD=Q11656377|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line}}, {{ill|lt=Ise Railway|Q11379148|WD=Q11379148|1=Ise Railway}} {{ill|lt=Ise Railway Ise Line|Q6079603|WD=Q6079603|lt=Ise Line|1=Ise Line}}) or {{ill|lt=Suzuka-shi Station|Q11649493|WD=Q11649493|1=Suzukashi Station}} ({{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Suzuka Line|Q900210|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line|1=Suzuka Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Mie Kōtsū|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus, get off at "Takaoka" stop, about 10 minutes on foot. ; Private car * Near Ichinomiya-chō intersection about 1 km south of Suzuka Ōhashi on {{ill|lt=National Route 23|Q5357579|WD=Q5357579|1=Japan National Route 23}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === {{commons|Category:Tsubaki Nakato-jinja}} ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|Q3541617|WD=Q3541617|1=Tsubaki Grand Shrine}} - another shrine claiming Ise ichinomiya. * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141|1=Sarutahiko Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suzuka, Mie]]
